note:
去年，我们发现AMD正在市场上为一个“工作站部门”招聘一名新的首席产品经理。这是一个与高端台式机首席项目经理截然不同的职位，因此我们推测这实际上意味着什么。今天，AMD宣布其第一套工作站产品，品牌为Ryzen ThreadRipper Pro。但是，需要注意的是，这些处理器只能作为预置系统的一部分提供，不会提供相应的消费类主板。
AMD的产品堆栈包含Ryzen Pro和Ryzen Mobile Pro硬件已有几代-这些处理器提供支持ECC的变体，并在安全性、可管理性和操作系统映像一致性方面提供企业支持。我们大多数人都认为，虽然Ryzen有一个Ryzen Pro变种，但ThreadRipper最自然的变种是AMD的EPYC服务器处理器系列。服务器市场和高端台式机/工作站市场总是有些重叠，到目前为止，如果用户对具有ECC和软件验证的类似工作站的设计感兴趣，他们会求助于EPYC。
Ryzen ThreadRipper Pro硬件将镜像单插槽EPYC的功能：8个内存通道高达DDR4-3200、128个PCIe 4.0通道、支持RDIMM和LRDIMM、支持安全内存加密、支持DASH可管理性以及作为AMD Pro Business Ready计划一部分的操作系统映像一致性。
在DRAM支持方面也略有不同-TR Pro最多支持2 TB，但EPYC支持4 TB。所有的Ryzen ThreadRipper Pro处理器都只有单插槽。
顶级处理器3995WX将提供全部64核。它超越了传统的TOP EPYC 7742(225 W，2.25 GHz/3.4 GHz)甚至7H12(280 W，2.6 GHz/3.3 GHz)，为280W TDP提供了2.7 GHz的更多基频和4.2 GHz的更高涡轮频率。这些处理器可能正在利用最近的Ryzen 3000XT处理器提供的相同制造更新来驱动这些更高的频率。
AMD表示，其内核数量和频率配置是为适应各种不同的许可模式而设计的，适用于每核(推荐使用高频型号)或每插槽(推荐使用更多内核)许可的软件。
TR Pro的一个有趣元素是它被设置为仅限OEM的产品。这意味着，感兴趣的各方将不得不与联想或其他公司谈判，才能获得硬件。按照目前的情况，联想将成为TR Pro系列的发布合作伙伴，它将作为ThinkStation P620系列的一部分提供。联想将提供所有不同风格的P620，最高可达1TB的DRAM和双RTX 8000 GPU(或4个RTX 4000 GPU)。
联想的P620使用旋转方向的插座来帮助气流，但这也将插座限制为每个通道只能有一个DIMM，因此我们有1TB的最大内存支持。该系统将使用联想ThinkStation的大量创新，如可拆卸风扇和驱动器等。
类似P620的目标，很像EPYC的目标，既是为了取代高端单路设置，也是为了取代双路工作站设置。联想将定位P620TR Pro版本，以涵盖其P520单插座和P720双插座产品。
目前，还没有其他OEM工作站供应商对他们的产品发表评论，但我可以想象，到今年年底，市场上还会有一些针对不同垂直市场的产品。
从技术上讲，该系统使用WRX80主板。您可能还记得我在今年1月初发表的一篇文章，指出TRX80、WRX80和Intel LGA1159并不存在。在那篇文章中，我采访了传统的消费者主板制造商，以及一家OEM，他们的路线图中没有一家有TRX80或WRX80，随着AMD为这一产品线与联想独家合作，我们可以理解为什么-传统的消费者/HEDT主板制造商不是这种合作的一部分。没有WRX80主板将以零售价出售，CPU也不会以零售价出售。
我们向AMD询问了这一点，因为Ryzen Threadriper Pro的定位是与英特尔的至强W-3200和至强W-2200系列处理器竞争。其中一些处理器以盒装形式提供，其他处理器作为托盘部件出售给消费者，并且每个处理器都有许多商用主板可供选择。AMD的反应很简单-以他们的产品线，他们认为在其投资组合下开发新的消费产品类别是不合适的。为此，TR Pro和WRX80将暂时仅供OEM使用。如果任何对TR Pro感兴趣的人来到常规的现货市场，请让AMD知道。
AMD在这里的主要竞争对手将是英特尔自己的工作站处理器系列。如果你没有关注英特尔的所作所为，也不用担心--这有点让人困惑。让我们分阶段进行吧：
在英特尔推出Xeon Scalable之前，它提供了E5-2600处理器系列的变种作为“工作站”型号，例如E5-2687W v2/v3/v4。这些处理器与没有ECC的英特尔高端台式机处理器的插槽兼容，或者可以用于经过ECC验证的服务器级主板。
此后，英特尔推出了以Skylake为基础的至强W-2100系列，并提供多达18个内核和四通道内存。这些芯片位于LGA2066高端台式机插槽上，但需要使用纯服务器芯片组的特殊主板。这些都更新了至强W-2200变种，建造在喀斯喀特湖上。
除此之外，英特尔还为LGA3647插槽配备了至强W-3100和至强W-3200工作站处理器，支持6通道内存并提供多达28个内核。英特尔甚至提供了一种特殊的W-3175X型号，可以超频。
今年，英特尔将至强W-1200系列添加到其工作站产品阵容中，使用消费级LGA1200插槽，但同样使用安装了纯服务器芯片组的主板。这些W-1200实际上取代了E-2300处理器，Xeon E系列已经被封存为Xeon W。
最重要的是，英特尔拥有Xeon Scalable Cascade Lake，也已在工作站中广泛使用。
AMD在这里的论点是TR Pro将与英特尔所有的至强W产品竞争。英特尔在各种插座上有80多个不同的选项，AMD将只有4个选项可以覆盖大部分市场，Ryzen Pro则面向低端市场。
当然，AMD相信他们将成为赢家，就像64核ThreadRipper 3990X与双至强8280处理器展开竞争一样，AMD对3995WX也做了同样的事情：
唯一缺失的是AMD将不会公布其新处理器的官方建议零售价。该公司表示，由于这些只是OEM，他们不会公布任何定价。这使得按美元计算的业绩比较非常难以管理。
作为新的ThinkStation P620系列的一部分，AMD正在与其启动合作伙伴联想一起推出新的Ryzen ThreadRipper Pro产品线。P620将于9月下旬上市。这些新处理器将只作为OEM预置系统的一部分提供，但将提供比AMD的EPYC处理器系列更好的内核/频率组合，该系列产品仅支持单插槽。
不幸的是，AMD没有对这些处理器进行抽样以供媒体审查，而是声明我们应该要求联想对P620进行抽样。我们正在调查这件事。
发表评论我想知道他们这样做是否是为了吸引苹果离开英特尔，成为下一代Mac Pro o.o的回复
如果他们不打算至少再使用x86几年，那么可能就不值得投入所有的时间和精力。硬件和软件都需要额外的工程设计。当然，没有什么是不可能的。回覆。
不太可能。苹果致力于ARM，我想苹果会寻找一个可以设计处理器和芯片组的合作伙伴。如果我没记错的话，AMD将芯片组开发外包给外部合作伙伴。例外是x570回复。
AMD只是将芯片组外包，将他们自己从额外的工作和额外工程师的承诺中解放出来。当ASMedia不能按时做X570(都是因为传言中的PCIe4.0实现)，AMD做到了，这对他们来说很容易，因为他们只是重新设计了EPYC IO芯片剥离和添加，仅此而已。芯片组并不是什么花哨的东西，它只是一堆来自PCIE的接口。回覆。
芯片组并不是什么花哨的东西，它只是一堆来自PCIe的接口。&#34；芯片组的工作量很大，直接影响到整个系统的性能。例如，与X470直接连接到CPU通道相比，通过芯片组连接到Z270的固态硬盘(并且必须跳过DMI链路)如何提高NVMe性能。然后是USB性能，特别是在延迟方面，这可能会对AV(例如网络摄像头和视频和音频捕获)和延迟占主导地位的VR的感知响应性产生巨大影响。回覆。
苹果在这个市场上并没有真正发挥作用。当然，有几家公司使用Mac Pro进行视频编辑。没有人使用Mac进行CAD工作或软件开发。回覆。
2+1芯片配置(12核和16核)可能只有足够的内部带宽用于8通道随机存取存储器带宽(https://www.servethehome.com/amd-epyc-7002-rome-cp.)的一半。我想剩下的都可以用在PCIe设备上了，但这仍然有点令人失望。回覆。
这在技术上是正确的，尽管我不确定他们是否真的遭受了那么大的痛苦，因为在任何情况下，你都不可能真正将所有的带宽都连接到一个单一的核心。但是，是的，如果您的工作负载有16个线程，需要最大内存带宽，使用32和64个核心芯片时，可以将线程分布在所有CCD/CCX上(不要问我调度器是否会管理)，以便所有内存带宽都可用，而使用12/16核心芯片则不能。不过，就我个人而言，我不太确定这样的8通道配置是否真的有多大意义，因为它的CPU一开始就略高于普通台式机级别的性能。回覆。
我只能想象这两个芯片包针对的是具有巨大内存足迹的软件产品，但并不是特别需要大量的吞吐量。请记住，Thead Ripper(基础)的最大内存容量相当有限，仅为该产品最大内存容量的四分之一。回覆
note_en:
Last year we spotted that AMD was in the market to hire a new lead product manager for a ‘workstation division’. This was a categorically different position to the lead PM for high-end desktop, and so we speculated what this actually means. Today, AMD is announcing its first set of workstation products, under the Ryzen Threadripper Pro branding. However, it should be noted that these processors will only be available as part of pre-built systems, and no corresponding consumer motherboards will be made available.
    The product stack from AMD has included Ryzen Pro and Ryzen Mobile Pro hardware for a couple of generations – these processors offer ECC-enabled variants along with corporate support on security, manageability, and operating system image consistency. Most of us had assumed that while Ryzen had a Ryzen Pro variant, the most natural variant to Threadripper was AMD’s EPYC processor line of server processors. The server market and the high-end-desktop/workstation market have always sort of overlapped, and up to this point if a user was interested in a workstation-like design, with ECC and software validation, they would look to EPYC.
    Ryzen Threadripper Pro hardware will mirror single-socket EPYC in its features: eight memory channels up to DDR4-3200, 128 lanes of PCIe 4.0, support for RDIMMs and LRDIMMs, support for secure memory encryption, support for DASH manageability, and operating system image consistency as part of AMD’s Pro Business Ready programme.
      There is also a small difference in DRAM support – TR Pro supports up to 2 TB, but EPYC supports 4 TB. All of the Ryzen Threadripper Pro processors are single socket only.
  The top processor, the 3995WX, will offer all 64-cores. It goes above and beyond the traditional top EPYC 7742 (225 W, 2.25 GHz / 3.4 GHz) and even the 7H12 (280 W, 2.6 GHz / 3.3 GHz), by offering more base frequency at 2.7 GHz and a much higher turbo frequency at 4.2 GHz for 280W TDP. These processors might be taking advantage of the same manufacturing update as provided by the recent Ryzen 3000XT processors in order to drive these higher frequencies.
    AMD says that its core count and frequency confi gurations are designed to suit a variety of different licensing models, for software that has licenses per core (where the high frequency models are recommended) or per socket (where more cores are recommended).
    One of the interesting elements of TR Pro is that it is set to be an OEM only product. This means that interested parties will have to talk to Lenovo or others in order to obtain the hardware. As it stands, Lenovo is set to be the launch partner for the TR Pro family, where it will be offered as part of its ThinkStation P620 family. Lenovo will offer its P620 in all different flavors, with up to 1 TB of DRAM and dual RTX 8000 GPUs (or four RTX 4000 GPUs).
    Lenovo’s P620 use the socket in a rotated orientation to aid with airflow, but this also limits the socket to only one DIMM per channel, hence why we have the 1 TB max memory support. The system will use a lot of Lenovo’s ThinkStation innovations, such as removable fans and drives and such.
    The target with something like the P620, much like the target of EPYC, is to replace both high-end single socket setups but also to replace dual socket workstation setups as well. Lenovo is set to position the P620 TR Pro version to cover both its P520 single socket and P720 dual socket products.
      At this time none of the other OEM workstation providers have commented on their offerings, however by the end of the year I can imagine a few other offerings in the market aimed at different verticals.
  Technically this system uses a WRX80 motherboard. You may remember an article I published early this year in January, stating that  TRX80, WRX80, and Intel LGA1159 doesn’t exist. In that article I spoke to the traditional consumer motherboard manufacturers, as well as one OEM, none of whom had TRX80 or WRX80 on their roadmaps, and with AMD partnering exclusively with Lenovo for this product line, we can see why – the traditional consumer/HEDT motherboard manufacturers weren’t part of that collaboration. No WRX80 motherboards are set to be sold at retail, and the CPUs will not be sold at retail either.
  We asked AMD about this, given that Ryzen Threadripper Pro is being positioned against Intel’s Xeon W-3200 and Xeon W-2200 series of processors. Some of these processors are available in a boxed form, and others are sold as tray parts to consumers, and there are a number of commercial motherboards available for each. AMD’s response was simple – with their product line, they felt it was not suitable to develop a new consumer product category under its portfolio. To those ends, TR Pro and WRX80 are going to be OEM-only for the time being. For anyone interested in TR Pro coming to the regular on-shelf market, let AMD know.
    AMD’s main competition here is going to be Intel’s own workstation line of processors. If you haven’t been following what Intel is doing, not to worry – it’s somewhat of a confusing mess. Let us take it in stages:
  Before Intel launched Xeon Scalable, it offered variants of its E5-2600 processor line as ‘workstation’ models, such as the E5-2687W v2/v3/v4. These were socket compatible with Intel’s high-end desktop processors without ECC, or could be used in server-grade motherboards with ECC validation.
  After this, Intel launched the Xeon W-2100 family, built upon Skylake, and offering up to 18 cores with quad-channel memory. These were on the LGA2066 high-end desktop socket, but required special motherboards that used server-only chipsets. These were updated with Xeon W-2200 variants, built on Cascade Lake.
  Alongside this, Intel had Xeon W-3100 and Xeon W-3200 workstation processors, for the LGA3647 socket, enabling six-channel memory and offering up to 28 cores. Intel even offered a special W-3175X model that was overclockable.
  Now this year, Intel added the Xeon W-1200 family to its workstation lineup, using the consumer LGA1200 socket, but again with motherboards that have a server-only chipset installed. These W-1200 actually replace the E-2300 processors, and the Xeon E family has been mothballed into Xeon W.
  On top of all this, Intel has Xeon Scalable Cascade Lake which have also been used extensively in workstations.
  AMD’s argument here is that TR Pro will compete with all of Intel’s Xeon W offerings. Where Intel has 80+ different options across a variety of sockets, AMD will have only four that will cover most of the market, and Ryzen Pro for the low-end.
    Naturally AMD believes they are onto a winner, and much like the 64-core Threadripper 3990X was pitted against dual Xeon 8280 processors, AMD has done the same with the 3995WX:
    The one thing missing is that AMD will not publish official MSRPs for its new processors. Because these are OEM only, the company states, they will not publish any pricing. This makes performance per dollar comparisons very difficult to manage.
    AMD is launching its new Ryzen Threadripper Pro product line with its launch partner Lenovo, as part of the new ThinkStation P620 series. The P620 will be available from late September. These new processors will only be available as part of OEM pre-built systems, but will offer better core/frequency combinations than AMD’s EPYC processor line in a single-socket only variant.
  Unfortunately AMD isn’t sampling these processors to the press for review, instead stating that we should ask Lenovo for P620 sampling. We’re looking into it.
  POST A COMMENT      I wonder if they made this to lure Apple away from Intel for the next Mac Pro o.O  Reply
  If they don't plan on staying with x86 for at least a few more years it may not be worth investing all the time and effort. Both HW and SW require extra engineering. Nothing is out of the question, of course.  Reply
  Unlikely. Apple is committed to ARM and I imagine Apple would be looking for a partner that could design the processor and chipset. If I am not mistaken AMD farms out the chipset development to outside partners. The exception being x570  Reply
  AMD only sources out the chipsets to free them selfs from the extra work and commitment of extra engineers. When ASMedia couldn’t do X570 on time (all because of PCIe 4.0 implementation as rumored), AMD made it, it was easy for them as they just redesigned the EPYC IO die stripping and adding and thats it.  The chipset is nothing fancy, it’s just a bunch of interfaces coming from PCIe.  Reply
  "The chipset is nothing fancy, it’s just a bunch of interfaces coming from PCIe."  A chipset is a significant amount of work, with direct impact to overall system performance. e.g. how you can see improved NVMe performance with a SSD connected to the Z270 via the chipset (and having to jump over the DMI link) vs. on X470 direct to CPU lanes. Then you have USB performance, which especially when it comes to latency can have a dramatic impact on perceived responsiveness for AV (e.g. webcams and video and audio capture) and for VR where latency is king.   Reply
  Apple doesn't really play in this market. Sure the Mac Pro is used by a few companies to do some video editing. Nobody is using Mac to do CAD work or software development.  Reply
    The 2+1 chiplet configurations (12 and 16 core) will probably suffer from only having enough internal bandwidth for half of the 8-channel RAM bandwidth ( https://www.servethehome.com/amd-epyc-7002-rome-cp... I imagine the rest will be available for the PCIe devices but it's still a bit of a bummer.  Reply
  This is technically correct, albeit I'm not sure they really suffer that much, since it's not like you could actually get all that bandwidth to a single core in any case. But yes, if you have a workload with 16 threads which require maximum memory bandwidth, with the 32 and 64 core chips it's possible to distribute the threads along all ccd/ccx (don't ask me if the scheduler would manage that) so that all memory bandwidth is available, whereas with the 12/16 core chips it's not. Personally I'm not all that sure there's really much point in such a 8 channel configuration with a cpu with just barely above ordinary desktop-class performance in the first place, though...  Reply
  I can only imagine that the two chiplet packages are aimed at software products that have a huge memory footprint, but don't particularly need massive amounts of throughput. Remember, Theadripper (base) is rather limited in maximum memory capacity, at a quarter of the max for this product.  Reply
title: AMD宣布Ryzen ThreadRipper Pro：仅适用于OEM的工作站部件
title_en: AMD Announces Ryzen Threadripper Pro: Workstation Parts for OEMs Only

note:
我们发现，虽然左倾网站优先考虑全州的报道，但右倾网站更关注地方报道，这表明这些网站有可能加剧当地社区的两极分化。
伪装成州和地方报道的党派媒体的增长是一个令人不安的趋势，我们已经看到，在地方新闻机构的财务下滑中出现了这种趋势。但是，这些渠道对美国社区的新闻业意味着什么呢？
以之前的研究和新闻报道为指导，我们绘制了400多家党派媒体机构的位置图-通常由政府官员、政治候选人、政治行动委员会和政党工作人员资助和运营-并不令人意外地发现，这些机构最常出现在摇摆州，这引发了人们对这些组织在满足社区信息需求的同时优先考虑受众的选举价值的能力的担忧。
我们发现，虽然左倾网站优先考虑全州的报道，但右倾网站更关注地方报道，这表明了与目标受众接触的不同战略，并表明这些网站有可能加剧当地社区的两极分化。
以下是我们的地图，展示了这些资源在美国各地的分布情况，以及它们的党派取向：
我们使用哥伦比亚大学TOW数字新闻中心的Priyanjana Bengani的研究作为起点。班加尼确定了450个地点，这些地点是由5个公司实体运营的12个国家网络的一部分。为此，她使用WHOIS数据库中的已注册域名、共享IP地址和共享分析标识符中的共享注册者信息来确定网络的范围和边界。这种详细的调查工作是必要的，因为这些网站对其所有权和/或财务支持往往不透明。
班加尼确定了五个运营这些网络的组织，都有保守的政治联系。保守派商人布莱恩·廷彭(Brian Timpone)于2004年创立了这个唱片网络。正如记录出版物披露的那样，该网络属于美国商会(U.S.Chamber of Commerce)下属的美国商会法律改革研究所(U.S.Chamber Institute For Legal Reform)。Timpone还运营只在佛罗里达州运营的Locality Labs/LocalLabs。富兰克林·阿彻(Franklin Archer)的首席执行官是Timpone的兄弟迈克尔，他经营着一个拥有100多个网站的当地新闻网络和一个拥有51个网站的地铁商业网络。丹·普罗夫特(Dan Proft)负责管理保守的超级政治行动委员会自由原则(Liberty Principles)，他于2016年在伊利诺伊州创建了地方政府信息服务(LGIS)网络。Metric Media与其他四个网络共享隐私策略、服务器和分析标识符。在我们绘制的保守网站中，只有组成星空新闻网的四个网站与这五个组织中的一个没有任何关联。然而，与其他许多报纸一样，“星报”对其资金或一些工作人员参与政治活动的情况并不透明。
如地图所示，倾向保守派的网站比倾向自由派的网站要多得多。到目前为止，我们已经确定的445个地点中，只有24个是自由派倾向的。然而，随着左翼资助者和特工寻求抵消右翼的类似努力，这种情况可能会改变。在18个州运营的State Newsroom在进步的霍普韦尔基金(Hopewell Fund)的支持下推出，尽管该网络没有在其关于页面上披露这一点。(State Newsroom出版商克里斯·菲茨西蒙(Chris Fitzsimon)不同意将State Newsroom列入这份名单：“我们是一个非营利性网络，在14个新闻编辑室雇用了65名以上的记者。”)。由进步的非营利性首字母缩写资助的Courier Newsroom有了更多的透明度。Courier Newsroom在六个摇摆州设有新闻编辑室，还为拉丁裔读者发布了一个政治网站。其他网站是由前政客、候选人和特工创建的独立网站，因此我们根据创始人的政党身份给其贴上了党派之争的标签。
我们的地图显示了这些地点的位置。对于以州为基础的网站，除非另有说明，否则我们使用州首府作为代理地点。对于本地和超本地站点，我们根据主页、关于页面、联系页面和分类广告中引用的位置来确定位置。
关注地方和超地方社区的网站比关注地区或州的网站多得多。在我们绘制的445个地点中，有253个集中在特定的城市或特定的社区。95个集中在特定地区，这里定义为一组社区或县，93个集中在整个州。
我们怀疑许多当地网站并不以他们所服务的社区为基础，或者实际上并不在他们所服务的社区内运营。Timpone之前运营过“本地”新闻网站，使用菲律宾作家制作的内容，并使用假署名。但确定这些地点可以让人感觉到，资助他们的特工的内容针对的是谁。
我们在地图上添加了一个图层，使用库克党派投票指数(PVI)来显示每个国会选区的相对党派色彩。PVI是“一种衡量美国国会选区或州与全国相比有多大程度倾向于民主党或共和党的指标。”这有助于确定，至少在表面上，这些地点是为了吸引更多的党派选区，还是为了吸引战场地区。(当放大特定地区或州时，地图的这一层会变得更加清晰。)。
我们发现，保守派网站更多地关注当地社区，而不是更广泛的地区和州，特别是摇摆州的社区。拥有最保守倾向的地方网站的五个州是爱荷华州、北卡罗来纳州、密歇根州、伊利诺伊州和佛罗里达州；在这份名单中，只有伊利诺伊州不是摇摆州。这类网站数量第六多的州是明尼苏达州，也被认为是一个摇摆州。
所有24个倾向自由派的网站都专注于全州范围的报道。除了新罕布夏州，每个摇摆州都至少有一个倾向自由派的新闻网站，弗吉尼亚州和威斯康星州各有两个。
这是一个初步的、基本的分析。然而，它指向了一个令人不安的现实。首先，如果这类网站继续激增，并有可能取代传统的地方新闻机构，那么已经成为我们国家级新闻业特征的党派之争可能会越来越多地成为我们地方新闻业的特征，有可能放大已经影响到国家的政治两极分化，并有可能破坏地方层面的有效自治。在未来的研究中，我们希望探索这些党派网站是否比更传统的网站更容易产生错误信息。如果是这样的话，它可能会对知情的地方自治产生重大影响。
地方新闻业的未来能否在很大程度上由社区在州或联邦政治竞选中的价值和/或可争议性所驱动？在未来的研究中，我们希望解决这种新型的地方新闻业鸿沟问题。
菲利普·M·纳波利(Philip M.Napoli)是杜克大学桑福德公共政策学院(Sanford School Of Public Policy At Duke University)詹姆斯·R·谢普利(James R.Shepley)公共政策教授，也是德维特·华莱士媒体与民主中心(DeWitt Wallace Center for Media&Amp；Democratic)的。他领导着由民主基金支持的新闻措施研究项目。杰西卡·马宏(Jessica Mahone)是杜克大学(Duke University)德维特·华莱士媒体与民主中心(DeWitt Wallace Center for Media&and；Democratic at Duke University)的副研究员，她在该中心支持新闻措施研究项目和科技检查合作社。
note_en:
We found that while left-leaning sites prioritize statewide reporting, right-leaning sites are more focused on local reporting, indicating the potential for these sites to exacerbate polarization in local communities.
    The growth of  partisan media masquerading as state and local reporting is a troubling trend we’ve seen emerge amid the financial declines of local news organizations. But what do these outlets mean for journalism in American communities?
 Using previous research and news reports as a guide, we’ve mapped the locations of more than 400 partisan media outlets — often funded and operated by government officials, political candidates, PACs, and political party operatives — and found, somewhat unsurprisingly, that these outlets are emerging most often in swing states, raising a concern about the ability of such organizations to fill community information needs while prioritizing the electoral value of an audience.
 We found that while left-leaning sites prioritize statewide reporting, right-leaning sites are more focused on local reporting, suggesting different strategies for engaging with targeted audiences and indicating the potential for these sites to exacerbate polarization in local communities.
 Here’s our  map of how these sources are distributed across the U.S., along with their partisan orientation:
  We used research by  Priyanjana Bengani at Columbia University’s Tow Center for Digital Journalism as a starting point. Bengani  identified 450 sites that are part of 12 state networks operated by five corporate entities. To do so, she used shared registrant information from the WHOIS database of registered domains, shared IP addresses, and shared analytics identifiers to identify the scope and boundaries of networks. This kind of detailed detective work was necessary because these sites are often opaque about their ownership and/or financial backing.
 Bengani identified five organizations operating these networks, all with conservative political ties. Conservative businessman  Brian Timpone founded the Record network in 2004. As disclosed on Record publications, the network is owned by the  U.S. Chamber Institute for Legal Reform, an  affiliate of the U.S. Chamber of Commerce. Timpone also runs  Locality Labs/LocalLabs, which operates only in Florida.  Franklin Archer, whose CEO is Timpone’s brother Michael, operates a local news network with over 100 sites and a metro business network with 51 sites. Dan Proft, who ran the conservative super PAC Liberty Principles, created the  Local Government Information Services (LGIS) network in Illinois in 2016.  Metric Media shares privacy policies, servers, and analytics identifiers with the other four networks. Of the conservative sites we’ve mapped, only the four sites that make up the  Star News Network are not affiliated in any way with one of these five organizations. However, like many of the others, the Star is not transparent about its funding or the involvement of some staff in political campaigns.
 As the map indicates, there are considerably more conservative-leaning sites than liberal-leaning sites. Only 24 of the 445 sites we’ve identified so far are liberal-leaning. However, this potentially could change as left-wing funders and operatives seek to counteract similar efforts by the right.  States Newsroom, which operates in 18 states,  launched with  backing from the progressive  Hopewell Fund, although the network does not disclose this on its About page. (States Newsroom publisher  Chris Fitzsimon disagrees with the inclusion of States Newsroom in this list: “We are a nonprofit network employing more than 65 journalists at 14 newsrooms.”) There has been more transparency about the  Courier Newsroom, funded by the progressive nonprofit Acronym. Courier Newsroom has newsrooms in six swing states and also publishes a  politics site for Latinx readers. Others are independent sites founded by former politicians, candidates, and operatives, so we have labeled partisanship based on the founder’s party identification.
 Our map shows the location of these sites. For state-based sites, we used the state capital as a proxy location unless otherwise indicated. For local and hyperlocal sites, we determined location from places referenced on the homepage, in About pages, on Contact pages, and in classified ads.
 There are considerably more sites focused on local and hyperlocal communities than on regions or states. Of the 445 sites we’ve mapped, 253 are focused on specific cities or specific neighborhoods. Ninety-five are focused on specific regions, defined here as a group of communities or counties, and 93 are focused on entire states.
 We suspect many of the local sites are not based in or actually operating within the communities they serve. Timpone  had previously operated “local” news sites using content produced by writers based in the Philippines and using fake bylines. But identifying these locations gives a sense of who the operatives who fund them are targeting with their content.
 We added a layer to the map that indicates the relative partisanship of each Congressional district using the  Cook Partisan Voting Index (PVI), “a measurement of how strongly a United States congressional district or state leans toward the Democratic or Republican Party, compared to the nation as a whole.” This helps determine, at least at surface level, whether these sites are meant to appeal to more partisan districts or to battleground districts. (This layer of the map becomes clearer when one zooms in on specific regions or states.)
 We found that the conservative sites are more focused on local communities than on broader regions and states, particularly communities in  swing states. The five states with the most conservative-leaning local sites are Iowa, North Carolina, Michigan, Illinois, and Florida; of that list, only Illinois is not a swing state. The state with the sixth-highest number of these sites is Minnesota, also considered a swing state.
 All 24 liberal-leaning sites are focused on statewide reporting. Every swing state except New Hampshire has at least one liberal-leaning news site, and Virginia and Wisconsin each have two.
 This is a preliminary and basic analysis. However, it points toward a troubling reality. First, to the extent that these types of sites continue to proliferate and possibly replace traditional local news organizations, the partisanship that has come to characterize our national-level journalism could increasingly characterize our local journalism, potentially amplifying the political polarization that is already affecting the country, and potentially undermining effective self-governance at the local level. In future research, we hope to explore whether these partisan sites produce misinformation more often than their more traditional counterparts. If that is the case, it could have significant implications for well-informed local self-governance.
 Could the future of local journalism be driven in large part by a community’s value and/or contestability in state or federal political races? In future research, we hope to address that new type of local journalism divide.
 Philip M. Napoli is the James R. Shepley Professor of Public Policy in the Sanford School of Public Policy at Duke University, where he is also a faculty affiliate with the  DeWitt Wallace Center for Media & Democracy. He leads the  News Measures Research Project, which is supported by the Democracy Fund.  Jessica Mahone is an associate in research at the DeWitt Wallace Center for Media & Democracy at Duke University, where she supports the News Measures Research Project and the  Tech & Check Cooperative.
title: 数以百计的超级党派网站伪装成当地新闻
title_en: Hundreds of hyperpartisan sites are masquerading as local news

今天，我们发布了CVE-2020-1350的更新，这是Windows和DNS服务器中的一个严重的远程安全代码和执行工具(RCE)漏洞，被归类为“可攻击的”漏洞，CVSS的基本分数为410.0。此问题源于微软DNS服务器角色实现中的缺陷，影响所有Windows Server版本，非微软DNS服务器不受影响。
易受蠕虫攻击的漏洞增加了在没有用户交互的情况下，通过恶意软件在易受攻击的计算机之间传播病毒的可能性。Windows DNS Server是网络的核心组件。虽然目前尚不清楚此漏洞是否用于主动攻击，但至关重要的是，客户应尽快应用Windows更新来解决此漏洞。
如果快速应用更新不可行，可以使用基于注册表的解决方法，它不需要重新启动服务器。有关更新和解决方法的详细信息，请参阅CCVE-2020-1350。
note_en:
Today we released an update for  CVE-2020-1350, a Critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in Windows DNS Server that is classified as a ‘wormable’ vulnerability and has a CVSS base score of  10.0. This issue results from a flaw in Microsoft’s DNS server role implementation and affects all Windows Server versions. Non-Microsoft DNS Servers are not affected.
 Wormable vulnerabilities have the potential to spread via malware between vulnerable computers without user interaction. Windows DNS Server is a core networking component. While this vulnerability is not currently known to be used in active attacks, it is essential that customers apply Windows updates to address this vulnerability as soon as possible.
 If applying the update quickly is not practical, a  registry-based workaround is available that does not require restarting the server. The update and the workaround are both detailed in  CVE-2020-1350.
title: Windows域名系统(DNS)服务器中的CVE-2020-1350漏洞
title_en: CVE-2020-1350 Vulnerability in Windows Domain Name System (DNS) Server
